# Inflation and the Phillips Curve {#sec-phillips-curve}

The Phillips curve — the relationship between unemployment and inflation — is one of the most debated relationships in macroeconomics. This chapter estimates it with real data and asks whether it still holds.

## A brief history of the Phillips curve

## Pulling the data

## The simple Phillips curve

## Expectations-augmented Phillips curve

## Has the Phillips curve flattened?

## Rolling regressions and time-varying parameters

## Exercises

1. Estimate a Phillips curve for the euro area using `readecb::ecb_hicp()` and `readecb::ecb_unemployment()`. Is the slope statistically significant?

2. Estimate a rolling 10-year Phillips curve for the UK. Plot the slope coefficient over time. When was the relationship strongest?

3. Add inflation expectations (from a survey or market measure) to the Phillips curve. Does the fit improve?
